🌙 Ramadan Athletic Performance Study¶

Daily Diary Analysis — N=12 Athletes, 30 Days¶

This notebook explores how fasting during Ramadan affects athletic performance across 12 athletes spanning 7 sports. We track energy levels, training completion, hydration status, sleep quality, and recovery scores across the full 30-day fasting period.

Dataset Variables

Column Description
Participant ID Athlete identifier (P01–P12)
Sport Basketball, Track, Soccer, Swimming, Volleyball, Tennis, Wrestling
Gender Male / Female
Age Athlete age
Training Intensity Low / Moderate / High
Day # Day of Ramadan (1–30)
Week Week block (1–5)
Training Completion Yes / Modified / No
Energy Level Self-reported 1–5 scale
Dehydrated? Yes / No
6+ hrs Sleep? Yes / No
Recovery Self-reported 1–5 scale

Notebook Sections

  1. Setup & Data Loading
  2. Dataset Overview
  3. Training Completion Analysis
  4. Energy & Recovery Trends Over Time
  5. Hydration & Sleep Impact
  6. Sport-by-Sport Breakdown
  7. Intensity Group Comparisons
  8. Gender Comparison
  9. Individual Athlete Profiles
  10. Key Insights Summary

1. Setup & Data Loading¶

In [24]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import seaborn as sns
from scipy import stats
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# ── Aesthetic config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
RAMADAN_PALETTE = {
    'primary':   '#1B4F72',   # deep navy
    'secondary': '#C0392B',   # deep red
    'accent':    '#F39C12',   # gold
    'green':     '#1E8449',
    'light':     '#AED6F1',
}
SPORT_COLORS = {
    'Basketball': '#E74C3C', 'Track': '#3498DB', 'Soccer': '#2ECC71',
    'Swimming':   '#9B59B6', 'Volleyball': '#F39C12', 'Tennis': '#1ABC9C',
    'Wrestling':  '#E67E22'
}

sns.set_theme(style='whitegrid', font_scale=1.05)
plt.rcParams.update({'figure.dpi': 120, 'axes.spines.top': False,
                     'axes.spines.right': False})

print('✅ Libraries loaded.')

✅ Libraries loaded.
In [25]:
import os
print(os.getcwd())

/Users/mustafaal-ogaili/Desktop/699 proj
In [26]:
# ── Load ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
RAW_PATH = 'Ramadan_Dataset.xlsx - Daily Diary Data.csv'

df_raw = pd.read_csv(RAW_PATH, skiprows=1)   # skip the title banner row

# ── Clean column names ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
df_raw.columns = [
    'participant_id', 'sport', 'gender', 'age', 'intensity',
    'day', 'week', 'date', 'completion', 'energy',
    'dehydrated', 'sleep_6hr', 'recovery'
]

# ── Type conversions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
df_raw['date']      = pd.to_datetime(df_raw['date'])
df_raw['energy']    = pd.to_numeric(df_raw['energy'],   errors='coerce')
df_raw['recovery']  = pd.to_numeric(df_raw['recovery'], errors='coerce')
df_raw['day']       = df_raw['day'].astype(int)
df_raw['week']      = df_raw['week'].astype(int)
df_raw['age']       = df_raw['age'].astype(int)

# ── Boolean helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
df_raw['dehydrated_bool'] = df_raw['dehydrated'].map({'Yes': True, 'No': False})
df_raw['sleep_bool']      = df_raw['sleep_6hr'].map({'Yes': True, 'No': False})
df_raw['completed_full']  = df_raw['completion'] == 'Yes'
df_raw['missed_session']  = df_raw['completion'] == 'No'

# ── Composite performance score (avg of energy + recovery, both /5) ───────────
df_raw['perf_score'] = (df_raw['energy'] + df_raw['recovery']) / 2

df = df_raw.copy()
print(f'✅ Data loaded: {len(df)} rows × {len(df.columns)} columns')
df.head()

✅ Data loaded: 360 rows × 18 columns

print('=== DATASET OVERVIEW ===')
print(f'  Athletes tracked   : {df.participant_id.nunique()}')
print(f'  Sports represented : {df.sport.nunique()}  →  {", ".join(sorted(df.sport.unique()))}')
print(f'  Days tracked       : {df.day.max()}')
print(f'  Total diary entries: {len(df)}')
print(f'  Gender split       : {dict(df.drop_duplicates("participant_id").gender.value_counts())}')
print(f'  Intensity split    : {dict(df.drop_duplicates("participant_id").intensity.value_counts())}')
print()
print('=== DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS (numeric columns) ===')
df[['energy', 'recovery', 'perf_score']].describe().round(3)

In [28]:
# Athlete roster with average metrics
roster = (
    df.groupby('participant_id')
    .agg(
        sport=('sport', 'first'),
        gender=('gender', 'first'),
        age=('age', 'first'),
        intensity=('intensity', 'first'),
        avg_energy=('energy', 'mean'),
        avg_recovery=('recovery', 'mean'),
        avg_perf=('perf_score', 'mean'),
        pct_full=('completed_full', 'mean'),
        pct_missed=('missed_session', 'mean'),
        pct_dehydrated=('dehydrated_bool', 'mean'),
        pct_slept=('sleep_bool', 'mean'),
    )
    .round(3)
    .reset_index()
)
roster['pct_full']      = (roster['pct_full']      * 100).round(1).astype(str) + '%'
roster['pct_missed']    = (roster['pct_missed']    * 100).round(1).astype(str) + '%'
roster['pct_dehydrated']= (roster['pct_dehydrated']* 100).round(1).astype(str) + '%'
roster['pct_slept']     = (roster['pct_slept']     * 100).round(1).astype(str) + '%'
print('=== ATHLETE ROSTER & AVERAGES ===')
roster

=== ATHLETE ROSTER & AVERAGES ===

3. Training Completion Analysis¶

Key question: How often did athletes complete, modify, or skip training — and does this change as Ramadan progresses?

In [29]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))

# ── Pie chart: overall completion distribution ────────────────────────────────
completion_counts = df['completion'].value_counts()
colors_pie = [RAMADAN_PALETTE['green'], RAMADAN_PALETTE['accent'], RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary']]
wedges, texts, autotexts = axes[0].pie(
    completion_counts,
    labels=completion_counts.index,
    autopct='%1.1f%%',
    colors=colors_pie,
    startangle=90,
    wedgeprops={'edgecolor': 'white', 'linewidth': 2}
)
for at in autotexts:
    at.set_fontsize(12)
    at.set_fontweight('bold')
axes[0].set_title('Overall Training Completion\n(All Athletes, 30 Days)', fontweight='bold')

# ── Stacked bar: completion by week ──────────────────────────────────────────
week_comp = (
    df.groupby(['week', 'completion'])
    .size()
    .unstack(fill_value=0)
    .reindex(columns=['Yes', 'Modified', 'No'])
)
week_comp_pct = week_comp.div(week_comp.sum(axis=1), axis=0) * 100
week_comp_pct.plot(
    kind='bar', stacked=True, ax=axes[1],
    color=[RAMADAN_PALETTE['green'], RAMADAN_PALETTE['accent'], RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary']],
    edgecolor='white', linewidth=0.8
)
axes[1].set_title('Training Completion by Week of Ramadan', fontweight='bold')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Week')
axes[1].set_ylabel('% of Sessions')
axes[1].set_xticklabels([f'Wk {w}' for w in week_comp_pct.index], rotation=0)
axes[1].legend(title='Completion', bbox_to_anchor=(1.01, 1), loc='upper left')
axes[1].yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter())

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_completion_overview.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print('→ Saved fig_completion_overview.png')

→ Saved fig_completion_overview.png
In [30]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5))

int_comp = (
    df.groupby(['intensity', 'completion'])
    .size()
    .unstack(fill_value=0)
    .reindex(columns=['Yes', 'Modified', 'No'])
)
int_comp_pct = int_comp.div(int_comp.sum(axis=1), axis=0) * 100

# reorder intensities
int_comp_pct = int_comp_pct.reindex(['Low', 'Moderate', 'High'])

int_comp_pct.plot(
    kind='bar', stacked=True, ax=ax,
    color=[RAMADAN_PALETTE['green'], RAMADAN_PALETTE['accent'], RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary']],
    edgecolor='white', linewidth=0.8, width=0.5
)
ax.set_title('Training Completion by Training Intensity', fontweight='bold', fontsize=14)
ax.set_xlabel('Training Intensity')
ax.set_ylabel('% of Sessions')
ax.set_xticklabels(['Low', 'Moderate', 'High'], rotation=0)
ax.legend(title='Completion', bbox_to_anchor=(1.01, 1), loc='upper left')
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter())
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_completion_intensity.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

4. Energy & Recovery Trends Over Time¶

Key question: Do energy and recovery scores decline, improve, or fluctuate across the 30-day fast?

In [31]:
daily = df.groupby('day').agg(
    energy_mean=('energy', 'mean'),
    energy_se=('energy', lambda x: x.sem()),
    recovery_mean=('recovery', 'mean'),
    recovery_se=('recovery', lambda x: x.sem()),
    perf_mean=('perf_score', 'mean'),
).reset_index()

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(14, 9), sharex=True)

for ax, metric, se_col, color, label in [
    (axes[0], 'energy_mean',   'energy_se',   RAMADAN_PALETTE['primary'],    'Energy Level'),
    (axes[1], 'recovery_mean', 'recovery_se', RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary'],  'Recovery Score'),
]:
    ax.fill_between(
        daily['day'],
        daily[metric] - daily[se_col],
        daily[metric] + daily[se_col],
        alpha=0.25, color=color, label='±1 SE'
    )
    ax.plot(daily['day'], daily[metric], color=color, lw=2.5, label=label)

    # rolling 7-day average
    rolling = daily[metric].rolling(7, center=True).mean()
    ax.plot(daily['day'], rolling, color='black', lw=1.5, ls='--', alpha=0.7, label='7-day rolling avg')

    # week dividers
    for wk_end in [7, 14, 21, 28]:
        ax.axvline(wk_end + 0.5, color='gray', lw=0.8, ls=':', alpha=0.7)
    ax.text(3.5,  ax.get_ylim()[0], 'Wk 1', fontsize=8, color='gray', ha='center')

    ax.set_ylabel(f'{label} (1–5)', fontsize=11)
    ax.set_ylim(1, 5)
    ax.legend(loc='upper right', fontsize=9)
    ax.set_title(f'{label} — Daily Group Average (N=12)', fontweight='bold')

axes[1].set_xlabel('Day of Ramadan', fontsize=11)

# add week labels
for i, (start, end) in enumerate([(1,7),(8,14),(15,21),(22,28),(29,30)], 1):
    mid = (start + end) / 2
    axes[0].text(mid, 1.05, f'Week {i}', fontsize=7.5, color='gray', ha='center')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_energy_recovery_trends.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
In [32]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))

for ax, metric, title in [
    (axes[0], 'energy',   'Energy Level by Ramadan Week'),
    (axes[1], 'recovery', 'Recovery Score by Ramadan Week'),
]:
    sns.boxplot(
        data=df, x='week', y=metric, ax=ax,
        palette=sns.color_palette('Blues_d', 5),
        width=0.55, fliersize=3
    )
    ax.set_title(title, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel('Week of Ramadan')
    ax.set_ylabel(f'{metric.capitalize()} (1–5)')
    ax.set_ylim(0.5, 5.5)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_weekly_boxplots.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
In [33]:
print('=== LINEAR REGRESSION: Day → Energy & Recovery ===')
for metric in ['energy', 'recovery']:
    slope, intercept, r, p, se = stats.linregress(df['day'], df[metric])
    direction = '📉 declining' if slope < 0 else '📈 improving'
    sig       = '✅ significant' if p < 0.05 else '❌ not significant'
    print(f'\n  {metric.upper()}')
    print(f'    slope = {slope:.4f}  |  R² = {r**2:.3f}  |  p = {p:.4f}')
    print(f'    Trend: {direction}  |  Statistical significance: {sig}')

=== LINEAR REGRESSION: Day → Energy & Recovery ===

  ENERGY
    slope = -0.0125  |  R² = 0.030  |  p = 0.0010
    Trend: 📉 declining  |  Statistical significance: ✅ significant

  RECOVERY
    slope = -0.0168  |  R² = 0.049  |  p = 0.0000
    Trend: 📉 declining  |  Statistical significance: ✅ significant

5. Hydration & Sleep Impact¶

Key question: Does being dehydrated or under-slept meaningfully hurt energy and recovery scores?

In [34]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(13, 9))

combos = [
    ('dehydrated', 'energy',   'Dehydration vs Energy Level',   axes[0, 0]),
    ('dehydrated', 'recovery', 'Dehydration vs Recovery',       axes[0, 1]),
    ('sleep_6hr',  'energy',   'Sleep ≥6 hrs vs Energy Level',  axes[1, 0]),
    ('sleep_6hr',  'recovery', 'Sleep ≥6 hrs vs Recovery',      axes[1, 1]),
]

for cat_col, metric, title, ax in combos:
    grp = df.groupby(cat_col)[metric].agg(['mean', 'sem']).reset_index()
    colors = [RAMADAN_PALETTE['green'], RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary']] if grp[cat_col].iloc[0] == 'No' \
             else [RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary'], RAMADAN_PALETTE['green']]
    bars = ax.bar(grp[cat_col], grp['mean'], yerr=grp['sem'],
                  color=colors, edgecolor='white', capsize=6, width=0.45)
    for bar in bars:
        h = bar.get_height()
        ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2., h + 0.05,
                f'{h:.2f}', ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_title(title, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_ylabel(f'{metric.capitalize()} (1–5)')
    ax.set_ylim(0, 5.5)

    # T-test annotation
    groups = [df[df[cat_col] == lbl][metric].dropna() for lbl in grp[cat_col]]
    t_stat, p_val = stats.ttest_ind(*groups)
    sig_text = f'p={p_val:.3f} {"*" if p_val < 0.05 else "(ns)"}'
    ax.text(0.98, 0.97, sig_text, transform=ax.transAxes,
            ha='right', va='top', fontsize=9, color='dimgray',
            bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.3', facecolor='lightyellow', alpha=0.7))

plt.suptitle('Impact of Hydration & Sleep on Athletic Performance Metrics',
             fontweight='bold', fontsize=14, y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_hydration_sleep.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
In [35]:
daily_dh = df.groupby('day').agg(
    dehydration_rate=('dehydrated_bool', 'mean'),
    sleep_rate=('sleep_bool', 'mean')
).reset_index()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 4))
ax.plot(daily_dh['day'], daily_dh['dehydration_rate'] * 100,
        color=RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary'], lw=2.5, label='% Dehydrated', marker='o', ms=4)
ax.plot(daily_dh['day'], daily_dh['sleep_rate'] * 100,
        color=RAMADAN_PALETTE['primary'], lw=2.5, label='% Got ≥6 hrs Sleep', marker='s', ms=4)
ax.axhline(50, color='gray', ls=':', lw=1, alpha=0.6)
for wk_end in [7, 14, 21, 28]:
    ax.axvline(wk_end + 0.5, color='gray', lw=0.8, ls=':', alpha=0.5)
ax.set_title('Daily Dehydration Rate vs. Sleep Adequacy Rate Over Ramadan', fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Day of Ramadan')
ax.set_ylabel('% of Athletes')
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter())
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_dehydration_sleep_timeline.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

6. Sport-by-Sport Breakdown¶

Key question: Which sports saw the biggest performance drop — or held up best — during Ramadan?

In [36]:
sport_agg = (
    df.groupby('sport')
    .agg(
        avg_energy   =('energy',          'mean'),
        avg_recovery =('recovery',         'mean'),
        avg_perf     =('perf_score',       'mean'),
        pct_dehydrated=('dehydrated_bool', 'mean'),
        pct_full     =('completed_full',   'mean'),
        pct_missed   =('missed_session',   'mean'),
    )
    .round(3)
    .sort_values('avg_perf', ascending=False)
)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(16, 5))

for ax, col, title in [
    (axes[0], 'avg_energy',   'Avg Energy Level'),
    (axes[1], 'avg_recovery', 'Avg Recovery Score'),
    (axes[2], 'avg_perf',     'Avg Performance Score'),
]:
    colors = [SPORT_COLORS[s] for s in sport_agg.index]
    bars = ax.barh(sport_agg.index, sport_agg[col], color=colors, edgecolor='white')
    ax.bar_label(bars, fmt='%.2f', padding=4, fontsize=10)
    ax.set_title(title, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlim(0, 5.5)
    ax.set_xlabel('Score (1–5)')

plt.suptitle('Average Performance Metrics by Sport', fontweight='bold', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_sport_comparison.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

In [37]:
sport_daily = df.groupby(['sport', 'day'])['perf_score'].mean().reset_index()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 5))
for sport, grp in sport_daily.groupby('sport'):
    ax.plot(grp['day'], grp['perf_score'], label=sport,
            color=SPORT_COLORS[sport], lw=1.8, alpha=0.85)

for wk_end in [7, 14, 21, 28]:
    ax.axvline(wk_end + 0.5, color='gray', lw=0.8, ls=':', alpha=0.5)

ax.set_title('Daily Performance Score Trend — By Sport', fontweight='bold', fontsize=13)
ax.set_xlabel('Day of Ramadan')
ax.set_ylabel('Avg Performance Score (1–5)')
ax.set_ylim(1, 5)
ax.legend(title='Sport', bbox_to_anchor=(1.01, 1), loc='upper left')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_sport_daily_trends.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

7. Intensity Group Comparisons¶

Key question: Do athletes training at higher intensities during Ramadan show worse outcomes?

In [38]:
intensity_order = ['Low', 'Moderate', 'High']

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5))

for ax, metric in [(axes[0], 'energy'), (axes[1], 'recovery')]:
    sns.violinplot(
        data=df, x='intensity', y=metric, order=intensity_order,
        palette=[RAMADAN_PALETTE['green'], RAMADAN_PALETTE['accent'], RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary']],
        inner='box', cut=0, ax=ax
    )
    ax.set_title(f'{metric.capitalize()} Distribution by Training Intensity', fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel('Training Intensity')
    ax.set_ylabel(f'{metric.capitalize()} (1–5)')
    ax.set_ylim(0.5, 5.5)

    # ANOVA
    groups = [df[df['intensity'] == i][metric].dropna() for i in intensity_order]
    f_stat, p_val = stats.f_oneway(*groups)
    ax.text(0.98, 0.97, f'ANOVA p={p_val:.3f}', transform=ax.transAxes,
            ha='right', va='top', fontsize=9, color='dimgray',
            bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.3', facecolor='lightyellow', alpha=0.7))

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_intensity_violin.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
In [39]:
int_miss = df.groupby(['intensity', 'week'])['missed_session'].mean().reset_index()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 4))
for intensity, grp in int_miss.groupby('intensity'):
    color = {'Low': RAMADAN_PALETTE['green'], 'Moderate': RAMADAN_PALETTE['accent'],
             'High': RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary']}[intensity]
    ax.plot(grp['week'], grp['missed_session'] * 100, marker='o', lw=2,
            color=color, label=intensity)

ax.set_title('Session Missed Rate by Week — Grouped by Training Intensity', fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Week of Ramadan')
ax.set_ylabel('% Sessions Missed')
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter())
ax.legend(title='Intensity')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_intensity_missed_rate.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

8. Gender Comparison¶

Key question: Are there meaningful differences between male and female athletes in how they manage fasting and training?

In [40]:
gender_daily = df.groupby(['gender', 'day']).agg(
    energy=('energy', 'mean'),
    recovery=('recovery', 'mean')
).reset_index()

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))

for ax, metric in [(axes[0], 'energy'), (axes[1], 'recovery')]:
    for gender, grp in gender_daily.groupby('gender'):
        color = RAMADAN_PALETTE['primary'] if gender == 'Male' else RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary']
        ax.plot(grp['day'], grp[metric], label=gender, color=color, lw=2)
    for wk_end in [7, 14, 21, 28]:
        ax.axvline(wk_end + 0.5, color='gray', lw=0.8, ls=':', alpha=0.5)
    ax.set_title(f'{metric.capitalize()} Over Time — Male vs Female', fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel('Day of Ramadan')
    ax.set_ylabel(f'{metric.capitalize()} (1–5)')
    ax.set_ylim(1, 5)
    ax.legend()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_gender_trends.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

# Summary table
gender_summary = df.groupby('gender').agg(
    avg_energy   =('energy',          'mean'),
    avg_recovery =('recovery',        'mean'),
    pct_dehydrated=('dehydrated_bool','mean'),
    pct_slept    =('sleep_bool',      'mean'),
    pct_missed   =('missed_session',  'mean'),
).round(3)
print('\n=== GENDER SUMMARY ===')
print(gender_summary)

=== GENDER SUMMARY ===
        avg_energy  avg_recovery  pct_dehydrated  pct_slept  pct_missed
gender                                                                 
Female       3.286         3.223           0.347      0.473        0.12
Male         3.228         3.090           0.448      0.548        0.11

9. Individual Athlete Profiles¶

A dashboard for each athlete showing their full 30-day arc of performance.

In [41]:
COMPLETION_COLORS = {'Yes': RAMADAN_PALETTE['green'],
                     'Modified': RAMADAN_PALETTE['accent'],
                     'No': RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary']}

fig, axes = plt.subplots(6, 2, figsize=(16, 40))
axes = axes.flatten()

for idx, pid in enumerate(sorted(df['participant_id'].unique())):
    p = df[df['participant_id'] == pid].copy()
    ax = axes[idx]

    sport    = p['sport'].iloc[0]
    gender   = p['gender'].iloc[0]
    age      = p['age'].iloc[0]
    intensity= p['intensity'].iloc[0]

    ax.fill_between(p['day'], p['energy'],   alpha=0.25,
                    color=RAMADAN_PALETTE['primary'],   label='Energy')
    ax.fill_between(p['day'], p['recovery'], alpha=0.25,
                    color=RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary'], label='Recovery')
    ax.plot(p['day'], p['energy'],   color=RAMADAN_PALETTE['primary'],   lw=1.8)
    ax.plot(p['day'], p['recovery'], color=RAMADAN_PALETTE['secondary'], lw=1.8)

    # mark completion status as scatter
    for status, grp in p.groupby('completion'):
        ax.scatter(grp['day'], [0.6] * len(grp), color=COMPLETION_COLORS[status],
                   marker='|', s=80, linewidths=2, zorder=5)

    ax.set_ylim(0, 5.5)
    ax.set_xlim(1, 30)
    ax.set_xlabel('Day', fontsize=9)
    ax.set_ylabel('Score (1–5)', fontsize=9)
    ax.set_title(
        f'{pid}  |  {sport}  |  {gender}, {age}  |  {intensity} intensity\n'
        f'Avg Energy: {p.energy.mean():.2f}  |  Avg Recovery: {p.recovery.mean():.2f}  '
        f'|  Missed: {int(p.missed_session.sum())} days',
        fontsize=9, fontweight='bold'
    )
    if idx == 0:
        ax.legend(fontsize=8, loc='upper right')

plt.suptitle('Individual Athlete Profiles — 30-Day Ramadan Performance Arc',
             fontweight='bold', fontsize=15, y=1.002)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_individual_profiles.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

10. Correlation Heatmap & Key Insights Summary¶

In [42]:
corr_df = df[['day', 'energy', 'recovery', 'perf_score',
              'dehydrated_bool', 'sleep_bool', 'completed_full', 'missed_session']].copy()
corr_df = corr_df.astype(float)

corr_matrix = corr_df.corr()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 7))
mask = np.triu(np.ones_like(corr_matrix, dtype=bool))
sns.heatmap(
    corr_matrix, mask=mask, annot=True, fmt='.2f',
    cmap='RdBu_r', center=0, vmin=-1, vmax=1,
    square=True, linewidths=0.5, ax=ax,
    cbar_kws={'shrink': 0.8}
)
ax.set_title('Correlation Matrix — Key Performance Variables', fontweight='bold', fontsize=13)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('fig_correlation_heatmap.png', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
In [43]:
print('=' * 60)
print('  📋 KEY INSIGHTS — RAMADAN ATHLETIC PERFORMANCE STUDY')
print('=' * 60)

# 1. Overall averages
print(f'\n1. OVERALL AVERAGES')
print(f'   Avg Energy Level  : {df.energy.mean():.2f} / 5.0')
print(f'   Avg Recovery Score: {df.recovery.mean():.2f} / 5.0')
print(f'   Avg Perf Score    : {df.perf_score.mean():.2f} / 5.0')

# 2. Training completion
comp_pct = df['completion'].value_counts(normalize=True) * 100
print(f'\n2. TRAINING COMPLETION')
for k, v in comp_pct.items():
    print(f'   {k:12s}: {v:.1f}%')

# 3. Hydration & sleep
dh_rate   = df['dehydrated_bool'].mean() * 100
sleep_rate = df['sleep_bool'].mean() * 100
print(f'\n3. WELLNESS METRICS')
print(f'   Reported dehydration : {dh_rate:.1f}% of athlete-days')
print(f'   Got ≥6 hrs sleep     : {sleep_rate:.1f}% of athlete-days')

# 4. Best & worst performers
print(f'\n4. BEST & WORST PERFORMING SPORTS (avg performance score)')
sport_perf = df.groupby('sport')['perf_score'].mean().sort_values(ascending=False)
print(f'   🥇 Best : {sport_perf.index[0]} ({sport_perf.iloc[0]:.2f})')
print(f'   🥈 2nd  : {sport_perf.index[1]} ({sport_perf.iloc[1]:.2f})')
print(f'   🔴 Worst: {sport_perf.index[-1]} ({sport_perf.iloc[-1]:.2f})')

# 5. Effect of dehydration
dh_yes = df[df['dehydrated'] == 'Yes']['energy'].mean()
dh_no  = df[df['dehydrated'] == 'No']['energy'].mean()
print(f'\n5. DEHYDRATION IMPACT ON ENERGY')
print(f'   Dehydrated days: {dh_yes:.2f}  |  Hydrated days: {dh_no:.2f}  |  Δ = {dh_no - dh_yes:.2f}')

# 6. Sleep impact
sl_yes = df[df['sleep_6hr'] == 'Yes']['recovery'].mean()
sl_no  = df[df['sleep_6hr'] == 'No']['recovery'].mean()
print(f'\n6. SLEEP IMPACT ON RECOVERY')
print(f'   ≥6 hrs sleep: {sl_yes:.2f}  |  <6 hrs sleep: {sl_no:.2f}  |  Δ = {sl_yes - sl_no:.2f}')

# 7. Trend
slope_e, _, _, p_e, _ = stats.linregress(df['day'], df['energy'])
slope_r, _, _, p_r, _ = stats.linregress(df['day'], df['recovery'])
print(f'\n7. 30-DAY TREND (linear regression slope per day)')
print(f'   Energy slope   : {slope_e:+.4f}  (p={p_e:.4f})')
print(f'   Recovery slope : {slope_r:+.4f}  (p={p_r:.4f})')

print()
print('=' * 60)

============================================================
  📋 KEY INSIGHTS — RAMADAN ATHLETIC PERFORMANCE STUDY
============================================================

1. OVERALL AVERAGES
   Avg Energy Level  : 3.25 / 5.0
   Avg Recovery Score: 3.15 / 5.0
   Avg Perf Score    : 3.20 / 5.0

2. TRAINING COMPLETION
   Yes         : 56.7%
   Modified    : 31.9%
   No          : 11.4%

3. WELLNESS METRICS
   Reported dehydration : 40.6% of athlete-days
   Got ≥6 hrs sleep     : 51.7% of athlete-days

4. BEST & WORST PERFORMING SPORTS (avg performance score)
   🥇 Best : Tennis (3.38)
   🥈 2nd  : Wrestling (3.24)
   🔴 Worst: Volleyball (3.02)

5. DEHYDRATION IMPACT ON ENERGY
   Dehydrated days: 3.18  |  Hydrated days: 3.30  |  Δ = 0.12

6. SLEEP IMPACT ON RECOVERY
   ≥6 hrs sleep: 3.23  |  <6 hrs sleep: 3.06  |  Δ = 0.17

7. 30-DAY TREND (linear regression slope per day)
   Energy slope   : -0.0125  (p=0.0010)
   Recovery slope : -0.0168  (p=0.0000)

============================================================

Notes for the App Team¶

The following derived metrics and data structures from this notebook are recommended as inputs to the app:

  • perf_score — composite average of energy and recovery (both on 1–5 scale). Simple and explainable.
  • Weekly aggregates — rolling up to week-level smooths daily noise and is suitable for trend cards.
  • Completion rate — Yes / (Yes + Modified + No) can feed a training adherence gauge.
  • Dehydration & sleep flags — binary signals that pair well with alert/badge UI patterns.
  • Sport and intensity facets — users may want to filter dashboards by these dimensions.

All saved chart images (fig_*.png) are in the same directory as this notebook.
